question_text: (a) Define (i) a complete metric space (ii) a first category set (iii) a Countable set (iv) an Open ball. (b) (i) Let X be a complete metric space and {On} be a countable collection of dense open subsets of X. Prove that that the union ⋃ On is not empty. (ii) Prove that in Rn, every family of disjoint non-empty open set is countable. (c) (i) Prove that in SRn the union of arbitrary collection of open sets is open. (ii) Prove that the finite intersection of a collection of open sets is open.
question_latex: (a) Define (i) a complete metric space (ii) a first category set (iii) a Countable set (iv) an Open ball. (b) (i) Let $X$ be a complete metric space and \(\{O_n\}\) be a countable collection of dense open subsets of $X\). Prove that the union ⋃ \(O_n\) is not empty. (ii) Prove that in \(\mathbb{R}^n\), every family of disjoint non-empty open set is countable. (c) (i) Prove that in \(\mathbb{R}^n\), the union of arbitrary collection of open sets is open. (ii) Prove that the finite intersection of a collection of open sets is open.

question_text: (a) Define the followings: (i) a function (ii) real-valued function (iii) vector-valued function. (b) Let A and B be metric spaces. Prove that f: A ⟼ B is continuous if and only if f −1(V) is an open set in A whenever V is open in B.
question_latex: (a) Define the followings: (i) a function (ii) real-valued function (iii) vector-valued function. (b) Let $A$ and $B$ be metric spaces. Prove that $f: X\rightarrow Y$ is continuous if and only if $f^{-1}(V)$ is an open set in $X$ whenever $V$ is open in $Y$.
